reviews.review_title: PUZZLE & TERROR IN A DISTURBING UNIVERSE
reviews.review_description:
THRILLS & MYSTERIES
A disturbing immersion into a world falling apart. Gentle horror, contemplative gameplay, psychological tension. Perfect for fans of unsettling VR narratives.
A VR THRILLER THAT WILL HAUNT YOU LONG AGO
Not One Less swept me away into a dimension of subtle dread where every room, whisper, and reflection seems to conceal a secret.
The game skillfully blends multi-perspective storytelling (up to seven perspectives depending on the chapter), metaphorical puzzles, and an oppressive atmosphere.
You are both an observer and a participant in a narrative that gradually twists with increasingly dark revelations.
Some players might be surprised by its less frenetic pace compared to horror blockbusters, but here the intensity comes from the atmosphere, the mounting tension, the anticipation.
In this context, the environments, lighting, and sound physics are very good, even if some textures or interactions still seem to be under development.
For an indie title, the promise is strong: a mature, immersive, and contemplative narrative VR experience.
If you enjoy being disturbed and pondering the experience even after playing, Not One Less deserves a place in your VR library.

A remarkably well-crafted narrative-driven game featuring a paranoid yet charismatic protagonist. Completing the story feels like watching a horror movie. While some moxels and animations are slightly rough, they don't diminish the game's unique appeal.

Not one less

A somewhat intriguing story wrapped up in an unusual format.

The scenes are mostly investigating intractable within the scenes and piecing together information to try and solve a puzzle before entering a discussion phase in co op to try answer a series of questions and to make sense of what happened (leading up to each scene)
Followed by a scene explaining what actually happened.

Its strange hybrid....imagine part point and click puzzle solving, part social deduction (or solo deduction if your playing alone) and part TV mini series.

There are some rough edges with some typos and word formatting but the game is in Early Access so I expect it to be tightened up before its full release.

But as it stand its a unusual but interesting "game" (I liken it to more of an experience) and the type of game that you should be checking out if your looking for something different, with an interesting and quit disturbing story.

reviews.review_description:
Let me start off by saying this is strictly a 18+ game… with its theme, nature of the story and gore.
The most mature story I’ve seen on the quest yet… be warned.

It’s a point and click style adventure/puzzle game, where you move around gathering clues to solve the puzzles to advance the story.

I’m aware this is an early access game, and there is small bits that need improving. Also would be better if it had smooth movement, Instead of point and click to move (teleport).

Sounds and music add to the spooky, freaky atmosphere.

Graphics are ok and serve their purpose.

Interested to see how this develops for the final release.

reviews.review_description: Game will not even allow you to start.  Friend and I both having the same issue.

This is a pretty intense game, pretty immersive despite all the dialogue being in Chinese.  You do get subtitles, for me they were about to close too my field of vision and need moving back a touch as they felt too close for comfort for me.  It is imperative you read the subtitles (unless you are fluent in Chinese!) as they hold the story and some clues you will need.  There was a mild bit of clipping as I leant through doors to see what was on the other side but, with the fact this is early release I shall let that go.  The game is very much teleport and snap turn with all the clues highlighted to collect although you will need to look at them to work out passwords etc.  I am only a few ‘chapters in’ and I am looking forward to seeing the dev progress with this one, well done this is a bit different…

reviews.review_description:
Tell me a game has a haunting feel to it & I just have to have it in my collection.
Totally different to anything I have ever played, completely all the dialogue is in Chinese with English subtitles, no issues with that but I did drift away from the text to look at the scenery, so I’m guilty of missing some of the story.
Very early to this so just an early impression on this.
Only snap turn available, hi would love free movement.
Good story so far. Will update as o go further in.
So far so good.
